title: docs: require explicit model change confirmation
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds a mandatory pre-edit confirmation gate to the model-management skill.
- Requires explicit approval of multiplier, paid status, GPU ownership, provider, and primary/fallback inference routes.
- Defines configured versus actually used provider/GPU semantics.
- Tracks the clean restart plan after closing #12342 and #12343.

Validation: `quick_validate.py .claude/skills/model-management`

gist.headline: Measure twice, route once
gist.blurb: The model garden now asks for a clear nod before anyone moves the irrigation pipes. Providers, GPUs, pricing status, and fallback routes all get checked before the edit lands.
gist.summary: Updated the internal model-management skill with a mandatory confirmation gate covering multipliers, paid status, GPU ownership, providers, and primary/fallback inference routes, while defining configured versus actually used infrastructure semantics.
gist.impact: Maintainers get a clearer pre-edit checklist for model routing changes, reducing ambiguity around which provider and GPU configuration will actually serve requests.
gist.visual_concept: A cozy model-routing garden map: Polly, wearing tiny librarian glasses and holding a checklist, pauses beside labeled primary and fallback water pipes feeding code-plants. A small CRT monitor robot points at two clearly marked routes, representing explicit confirmation before changing provider or GPU settings.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the cute yellow-and-brown striped bee mascot wearing tiny librarian glasses, carefully checking a clipboard beside a garden of code-plants fed by labeled primary and fallback irrigation pipes; a cheerful CRT monitor robot points at a routing map. Prominent lime green (#ecf874) pipes and glow, warm cream and lavender pastels, soft flowers and vines, cozy pixel art, 8-bit aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, with pixel-font text: "CONFIRM ROUTE".
